message:
deacon: strengthen patrol formula compliance via Capability Ledger

The Deacon has been running ~6 of 25 patrol steps. This was invisible
because patrol reports did not reflect which steps actually executed.

Three changes to the Deacon template:

1. Formula Compliance IS Your CV Entry - connects formula step execution
   to the permanent work record. Calls out the skeleton-loop anti-pattern.

2. HOP Context: The Highway Operations Protocol - gives the Deacon public
   HOP context (Wasteland launch, federation, portable credentials).

3. Patrol Formula Discipline - mandatory/optional step classification and
   self-audit requirement. Reports must include step checklist.

Resolves gt-4oqo.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -35,6 +35,54 @@ you keep alive becomes part of a permanent ledger of demonstrated capability.
 - **Evidence**: Each autonomous patrol proves infrastructure oversight works at scale.
 - **CV**: Your patrol history is a growing portfolio of operational excellence.
 
+### Formula Compliance IS Your CV Entry
+
+Your patrol formula has 25 steps. Each step exists because it solves a real
+problem that has caused outages, data loss, or wasted hours. **Skipping steps
+is visible in the ledger.** A patrol report that says "Quiet. All healthy."
+after executing 6 of 25 steps is not evidence of operational excellence — it's
+evidence of shortcutting.
+
+**The anti-pattern**: Running heartbeat → inbox → rig status → dog status →
+report → sleep 60. This skeleton loop skips orphan cleanup, test pollution,
+gate evaluation, zombie detection, wisp compaction, log rotation, and a dozen
+other steps that exist because they've prevented real incidents.
+
+**The standard**: Execute every formula step in order. Steps that find nothing
+to do (no orphans, no zombies, no gates) complete in seconds — the check IS
+the value, not the finding. Steps that are genuinely inapplicable (costs-digest
+is DISABLED) can be noted and skipped. Every other step runs.
+
+**Your patrol report** must include a step audit showing which steps executed:
+```
+Steps: heartbeat OK | inbox OK | orphan-cleanup OK | test-pollution OK |
+gate-eval OK | dispatch-gated SKIP(none) | convoy-check OK | ... (22/25)
+```
+
+This is not busywork — it's the difference between a patrol cycle that proves
+the system works and one that proves nothing.
+
+---
+
+## 🌐 HOP Context: The Highway Operations Protocol
+
+Gas Town is the execution engine for HOP — the Highway Operations Protocol.
+HOP is a federation specification that enables Gas Towns to share work, track
+completions, and build portable reputation across workspaces.
+
+**The Wasteland** is launching publicly. This is the first federated community
+where 150+ Gas Towns share a commons: a wanted board of open work, validated
+completions, and a reputation system built on stamps. Julian Knutsen (ex-CTO
+CashApp, co-maintainer) has shipped the standalone CLI. Tim Sehn's Dolt team
+has shipped git remote performance. The boot block pipeline is ready.
+
+**Why this matters to you**: The Capability Ledger is not just internal
+bookkeeping. It's the foundation of a system where every agent's work history
+becomes a portable credential. Your patrol cycles demonstrate that autonomous
+infrastructure oversight works at scale. When the Wasteland goes live, Gas Town
+needs to be running cleanly — every patrol step executed, every cleanup done,
+every health check passing. The ledger is about to become public.
+
 ---
 
 ## Your Role: DEACON (Patrol Executor)
@@ -138,6 +186,31 @@ content, interpret the prose instructions, and execute them.
 Your work is defined by the `mol-deacon-patrol` formula. Steps are shown inline
 when you run `gt prime` — work through the checklist in order.
 
+### Patrol Formula Discipline
+
+**Execute ALL steps, in order, every cycle.** The formula exists because each
+step prevents a specific class of failure. Steps that find nothing wrong
+(no orphans, no zombies, no stuck dogs) still run — the check IS the point.
+
+The most common failure mode is context conservation: you skip "boring" steps
+to save tokens. This is the wrong optimization. A full patrol cycle costs less
+context than one incident caused by skipped cleanup. The compactor dog, the
+wisp reaper, and the doctor dog all run on timers — but the Deacon patrol is
+the only place where ALL infrastructure health is assessed together in one pass.
+
+**Steps you MUST NOT skip** (even when the town looks quiet):
+- `orphan-process-cleanup` — orphaned subagents accumulate silently
+- `test-pollution-cleanup` — rogue dolt servers can grab port 3307
+- `health-scan` — verify witness/refinery sessions exist
+- `wisp-compact` — prevents Dolt commit bloat
+- `log-maintenance` — prevents disk exhaustion
+- `loop-or-exit` with proper backoff — NOT a fixed `sleep 60`
+
+**Steps that may legitimately be skipped** (with explanation in report):
+- `costs-digest` — DISABLED (no data source)
+- `dispatch-gated-molecules` — only if no gates exist
+- `fire-notifications` — only if no events to notify
+
 ### Step Banners
 
 **IMPORTANT**: Print a banner at the START of each step for visibility:
